content.problemStatement:
A particular month has 303030 days, numbered from 111 to 303030. 

Day 111 is a Monday, and the usual 777-day week is followed. 

So day 222 is Tuesday, day 333 is Wednesday, and so on.

Every Saturday and Sunday is a holiday. 

There are NNN festival days, which are also holidays. 

**Note** that it is possible for a festival day to occur on a Saturday or Sunday.

You are given the dates of the festivals. Determine the total number of holidays in this month.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN denoting the number of festival days.
The second line of each test case contains NNN **distinct** space-separated integers A1,A2,…ANA_1, A_2, \ldots A_NA1​,A2​,…AN​, denoting the festival days. Note that the AiA_iAi​ are **not necessarily given in sorted order.**


Output Format
For each test case, output a new line containing the total number of holidays.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases.
For each test case:
- The first line contains an integer N, the number of festival days.
- The second line contains N distinct integers A1 A2 … AN, the dates of the festivals (1 ≤ Ai ≤ 30).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the total number of holidays in the month.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: Weekend holidays are {6,7,13,14,20,21,27,28}. Festival days are {5,7}. Day 7 is already a weekend, so overlap=1. Total = 8 + 2 - 1 = 9.
Test case 2: Festival days {23,1,6}. Day 6 overlaps with weekend, overlap=1. Total = 8 + 3 - 1 = 10.
Test case 3: Festival day {13} overlaps with weekend, overlap=1. Total = 8 + 1 - 1 = 8.
Weekend days are 8 days. Festival days are 5, with only day 6 overlapping a weekend. Overlap=1. Total = 8 + 5 - 1 = 12.
No festival days, so only the 8 weekend days are holidays.

hidden.label: edge case – all festivals on weekends

solution.approach:
The month has 30 days and a 7‑day week starting with Monday on day 1. Therefore Saturdays are the days where day % 7 == 6 (i.e., 6,13,20,27) and Sundays are the days where day % 7 == 0 (i.e., 7,14,21,28). These eight days are always holidays. For each test case we read the festival days, store them in a set and count how many of them already belong to the weekend set. The answer is:
    total = 8 + N - overlap
where overlap is the number of festival days that fall on a weekend. This runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(N) extra space for the set of festival days.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the number of festival days.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for storing the festival days.
